title: How do you undertake a lip enhancement with a cleft lip？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: How do you undertake a lip enhancement with a cleft lip？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: How do you do a lip enhancement with a cleft lip so cleft lips are unique so they're a little bit like any scar [Music] each one needs to be assessed differently the ones that i've treated the most common issue that you face is that there simply isn't room where the scar is to ex…

segments.text: how do you do a lip enhancement with a cleft lip so cleft lips are unique so they're a little bit like any scar [Music] each one needs to be assessed differently the ones that i've treated the most common issue that you face is that there simply isn't room where the scar is to expand that much so you're trying to generally nudge the tissue in the right direction in those places where there's scarring so that you can kind of smooth any imperfections just to make them look a little bit fuller but if this is true with any scar by the way that if you then add too much volume the scar may not release its tension on the tissue as well as other tissue does so effectively have a tight band where the scar is and that does limit how much volume you can put in but that's true with any scar and the most common i do see on lips is a cleft palate that's been repaired at left lip um and so you just have to do things slowly and look at it as you go on you can't do a treatment plan and just apply it you need to get that feedback while you're injecting to see how it's going to go and of course you need to consent and consult your patients well so that they

understand the limitations but you can definitely make a really good difference i've done loads in the past that have made a lovely difference the patient's well-being and i'm sure you can do that so it's certainly not a contraindication some people think it's a contraindication but you do need to think carefully and go slowly so hope that helps

transcript: how do you do a lip enhancement with a cleft lip so cleft lips are unique so they're a little bit like any scar [Music] each one needs to be assessed differently the ones that i've treated the most common issue that you face is that there simply isn't room where the scar is to expand that much so you're trying to generally nudge the tissue in the right direction in those places where there's scarring so that you can kind of smooth any imperfections just to make them look a little bit fuller but if this is true with any scar by the way that if you then add too much volume the scar may not release its tension on the tissue as well as other tissue does so effectively have a tight band where the scar is and that does limit how much volume you can put in but that's true with any scar and the most common i do see on lips is a cleft palate that's been repaired at left lip um and so you just have to do things slowly and look at it as you go on you can't do a treatment plan and just apply it you need to get that feedback while you're injecting to see how it's going to go and of course you need to consent and consult your patients well so that they understand the limitations but you can definitely make a really good difference i've done loads in the past that have made a lovely difference the patient's well-being and i'm sure you can do that so it's certainly not a contraindication some people think it's a contraindication but you do need to think carefully and go slowly so hope that helps
